title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Mastery Check
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a dead-code and dependency audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Unused Symbols · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Unused Symbols without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.

content.text: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Unused Symbols, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a dead-code and dependency audit.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prefixing every unused value with an underscore fixes dead code. This fails the Unused Symbols contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Safe Removal applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
